reasoning: SPY at 658.39 has gapped up +5.19 overnight and is currently mid-premarket-range. The ribbon is BULL (fast/pivot above slow, 115¢ spread). The critical battle level is 660.56 (today's premarket high, Active, 2★) only +2.17 above. This is the overhead ceiling. Support sits at 657.47 (premarket low, Active, 2★) just -0.92 below. Asymmetry favors bulls: room to 660.56 resistance (+2.17) exceeds room to 657.47 support (-0.92). If 660.56 holds, target is the 5-day high at 669.72. If 660.56 breaks, first retest of 657.47 is the floor, with gap-fill target at 653.2 (prior close) much further down. VIX 26.22 HIGH but falling (-2.67%), suggesting volatility may compress as session opens. Ribbon stack alignment (BULL) confirms upside bias. Confidence is moderate because three Active-tier levels (660.56, 657.47, 657.03) have equal strength — clear level structure but price is in the zone, not at a dominant pivot.

secondary.condition: if SPY bounces at 657.47 (PML) on any dip

invalidation.condition: if SPY breaks below 657.47 (PML) with volume

key_observations: SPY has gap-closed +$5.19 overnight, establishing today's premarket high at 660.56 — the immediate resistance battle level.
Ribbon stack is cleanly BULL with 115¢ spread (fast 658.3 > pivot 658.17 > slow 657.15), supporting upside bias and confirming gap legitimacy.
Asymmetry favors upside: +2.17 to resistance (660.56) vs. -0.92 to support (657.47), with much more room beyond PMH if it breaks.
VIX 26.22 is HIGH but declining (-2.67%), indicating potential volatility compression as RTH opens — bulls may have easier path.
Three equal-strength Active levels (all 2★) cluster here, creating a well-defined battle zone rather than a single dominant pivot; confidence moderately high but not extreme.
